briefdescription.para: Size of NIST P-384 curve in bytes.
Size of NIST P-521 curve in bytes.
Size of NIST P-256 curve in bytes.
Size of NIST P-192 curve in bytes.
Size of NIST P-224 curve in bytes.
Size of NIST P-521 curve is 66 bytes.

detaileddescription.para: This macro is used to make the address word aligned

briefdescription.para: Operates APIs on little endian format.
This function generates Public Key for a given curve type.

parameterdescription.para: Is a type of elliptic curve

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_NON_SUPPORTED_CRV - When elliptic Curve is not supported
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
Generate public key with provided private key and curve type

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_EllipticCrvTyp CrvType, const u8 *Hash, const u32 HashLen, const u8 *D, const u8 *K, const XSecure_EllipticSign *Sign)
memberdef.name: XSecure_EllipticGenerateSignature

briefdescription.para: This function generates signature for a given hash and curve type.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the hash for which sign has to be generated
Length of the hash in bytes
Pointer to the static private key

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument

simplesect.para: K, the ephemeral private key, shall be an unpredictable (cryptographically secure) random number unique for each signature Note that reuse or external predictability of this number generally breaks the security of ECDSA

listitem.para: Generate signature with provided hash, private key, ephemeral key and curve type

briefdescription.para: This function validates the public key for a given curve type.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
Validate the public key for a given curve type

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_EllipticCrvTyp CrvType, const u8 *Hash, const u32 HashLen, const XSecure_EllipticKey *Key, const XSecure_EllipticSign *Sign)
memberdef.name: XSecure_EllipticVerifySign

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the signature for a given hash, key and curve type.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the hash for which sign has to be generated

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
Verify signature with provided hash, public key and curve type

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_EllipticCrvTyp CrvType, const u64 DAddr, const XSecure_EllipticKeyAddr *KeyAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_EllipticGenerateKey_64Bit

briefdescription.para: This function generates Public Key for a given curve type using private key where both keys located at 64 bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Is a type of elliptic curve

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_NON_SUPPORTED_CRV - When elliptic Curve is not supported
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_GEN_KEY_ERR - Error in generating Public key
Store Priv key to local buffer
Place the hardware core into the reset
Generate public key with provided private key and curve type
Store key to destination address

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_EllipticCrvTyp CrvType, const XSecure_EllipticHashData *HashInfo, const u64 DAddr, const u64 KAddr, const XSecure_EllipticSignAddr *SignAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_EllipticGenerateSignature_64Bit

briefdescription.para: This function generates signature for a given hash and curve type where data is located at 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to Hash Data i.e. Hash Address and length
Address of the static private key

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_NON_SUPPORTED_CRV - If curve data pointer is NULL
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_GEN_SIGN_BAD_RAND_NUM - When Bad random number used for sign generation
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_GEN_SIGN_INCORRECT_HASH_LEN Incorrect hash length for sign generation
XST_FAILURE - On any other failures

simplesect.para: K, the ephemeral private key, shall be an unpredictable (cryptographically secure) random number unique for each signature Note that reuse or external predictability of this number generally breaks the security of ECDSA

listitem.para: Store Hash,D,K to local buffers
Generate signature with provided hash, private key, ephemeral key and curve type.
Store Sign to destination address
Zeroize local key copy

briefdescription.para: This function validates the public key for a given curve type where key is located at 64-bit address.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_NON_SUPPORTED_CRV - If curve data pointer is NULL
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_KEY_ZERO - When Public key is zero
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_KEY_WRONG_ORDER Wrong order of Public key
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_KEY_NOT_ON_CRV - When Key is not found on the curve
XST_FAILURE - On any other failures
Store Pub key(Qx,Qy) to local buffer
Validate the public key for a given curve type

memberdef.argsstring: (XSecure_EllipticCrvTyp CrvType, const XSecure_EllipticHashData *HashInfo, const XSecure_EllipticKeyAddr *KeyAddr, const XSecure_EllipticSignAddr *SignAddr)
memberdef.name: XSecure_EllipticVerifySign_64Bit

briefdescription.para: This function verifies the signature for a given hash, key and curve type where data is located at 64-bit address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to Hash Data i.e. Hash Address and length

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS - On success
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_INVALID_PARAM - On invalid argument
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_NON_SUPPORTED_CRV - If curve data pointer is NULL
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_BAD_SIGN - When signature provided for verification is bad
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_VER_SIGN_INCORRECT_HASH_LEN Incorrect hash length for sign verification
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_VER_SIGN_R_ZERO R set to zero
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_VER_SIGN_S_ZERO S set to zero
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_VER_SIGN_R_ORDER_ERROR R is not within ECC order
XSECURE_ELLIPTIC_VER_SIGN_S_ORDER_ERROR S is not within ECC order
XST_FAILURE - On any other failures
Store Pub key(Qx,Qy) and Sign(SignR, SignS) to local buffers
Store Hash to local buffer
Verify signature with provided hash, public key and curve type

briefdescription.para: This file contains the interface functions for ECC engine.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vns 03/27/19 First Release 4.0 vns 08/24/20 Updated file version to sync with library version 4.2 har 11/07/19 Typo correction to enable compilation in C++ rpo 04/02/20 Added crypto KAT APIs 4.3 har 08/24/20 Added function prototype for APIs to generate and verify ECDSA public key and signature am 09/25/20 Resolved MISRA C violations har 10/12/20 Addressed security review comments har 10/14/20 Replaced ecdsa with elliptic in names of function and macros 4.5 har 01/18/21 Updated prototype for XSecure_EllipticKat 4.6 har 07/14/21 Fixed doxygen warnings gm 07/16/21 Added support for 64-bit address 5.0 kpt 07/24/22 Moved XSecure_EllipticKat into xsecure_kat.c 5.2 yog 05/18/23 Updated the flow for Big Endian ECC Mode setting yog 06/07/23 Added support for P-256 Curve mmd 07/09/23 Included header file for crypto algorithm information am 08/18/23 Added XSecure_EllipticGetCrvSize() prototype vss 09/11/2023 Fixed MISRA-C Rule 8.13 violation 5.4 yog 04/29/24 Fixed doxygen grouping and doxygen warnings. mb 05/23/24 Added support for P-192 Curve mb 05/23/24 Added support for P-224 Curve 5.7 tbk 03/30/26 Remove unwanted header
